title: The Softmax Function
concept: The Softmax function converts raw network outputs (logits) into a probability distribution that sums to 1.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the output of the final linear layer in a neural network before activation called?
exercises.explain: Correct! Raw, unnormalized outputs of the final layer are called logits.

cards.title: Sharing a pizza
cards.body: Imagine you have raw points for three friends. If you want to split a pizza based on these points, you must convert the points into percentages that sum to 100%. Softmax does exactly this for model outputs.

cards.title: What is Softmax?
cards.body: The Softmax function takes a vector of raw scores (logits) and exponentials each score, then normalizes them by dividing by the sum of all exponentials. This creates a valid probability distribution where each value is between 0 and 1, and they all sum to 1.

cards.title: A tug-of-war for confidence
cards.body: Think of Softmax as a tug-of-war. If one class's score increases, it pulls confidence away from the other classes because the total probability must always sum to 100%.

cards.title: Calculating Softmax
cards.body: If logits are [2.0, 1.0, 0.1], applying exponentials gives [7.39, 2.72, 1.11]. Summing these yields 11.22. Dividing each by 11.22 gives probabilities: [0.66, 0.24, 0.10].

cards.title: Logits are not raw percentages
cards.body: You might think a logit score of 10 is twice as likely as a score of 5. In reality, because Softmax uses exponentials, a score of 10 is e^5 (about 148 times) more likely than a score of 5!

cards.title: Logits to Probabilities
cards.body: How Softmax squashes and normalizes numbers:

left.text:
Scores: [2.0, 1.0, -1.0]
Can be positive or negative
Do not sum to anything specific

right.text:
Probabilities: [0.70, 0.26, 0.04]
All values between 0 and 1
Sums exactly to 1.0

exercises.prompt: What mathematical operation does Softmax apply to logits before normalising them?
exercises.explain: Softmax exponentials each score, which ensures all resulting values are strictly positive.

options.text: Exponentiation (e raised to the power of the score)

exercises.prompt: True or false: The outputs of a Softmax function applied to any vector of numbers will always sum to 1.
exercises.explain: True. The denominator of the Softmax function is the sum of the numerators, forcing the sum of outputs to equal 1.
A probability distribution means a set of probabilities that sum to 1.0.
exercises.sentence: Softmax converts logits into a valid probability ___.

exercises.prompt: Which of these vectors could represent the output of a Softmax activation?
exercises.explain: Only [0.7, 0.2, 0.1] consists of values between 0 and 1 that sum exactly to 1.0.

exercises.prompt: If a model outputs logits [3.0, 3.0, 3.0] for three classes, what will the Softmax probabilities be?

options.text: [0.33, 0.33, 0.33], because all logits are equal and the sum must be 1.
[0.9, 0.05, 0.05], because the first class takes priority.

exercises.explain: Exactly! When logits are identical, the exponentials are identical, leading to an equal probability split of 1/3 each. Next, let's see how we measure error using these probabilities!
